div1.entryFree:
Pervert, 1) to turn another way, to avert: let's follow him and p. the present wrath he hath against himself, Cymb. II, 4, 151.
2) to lead astray, to corrupt, to seduce: and new p. a reconciled maid, Compl. 329. trust not my holy order, if I p. your course, Meas. IV, 3, 153. he hath --ed a young gentlewoman, All's IV, 3, 17.
